headline: Applied Materials Q2 FY26 blowout: record rev $7.91B, EPS $3.51; lifts 2026 WFE growth to >30% from >20%; Q3 guide $8.95B vs $8.09B; shares +8% after hours
summary: AMAT printed record Q2 FY26 after the bell May 14: revenue $7.91B (+11% YoY) vs $7.82B consensus; GAAP EPS $3.51 (+33% YoY) vs $2.71 consensus; non-GAAP EPS $2.86. GAAP gross margin 49.9% (25-year high). CEO Gary Dickerson lifted full-year 2026 semiconductor equipment growth to >30%, up from prior >20% guide issued in February. Q3 FY26 guide: rev $8.95B +/- $0.5B (vs $8.09B Street); non-GAAP EPS $3.36 +/- $0.20 (vs $2.88 Street). Capital allocation: 15% dividend hike to $0.53/qtr, agreed to acquire ASMPT's NEXX advanced-packaging business, new EPIC center partnerships. Analyst reaction: Lynx Equity to $540 (from $440) citing NAND/leading-edge logic acceleration through 2028; Citi already at $520; Morningstar's William Kerwin called it a strengthening AI-fueled WFE upcycle. Read-throughs: LRCX, KLAC, ASML, ENTG, HBM names (000660.KS, 005930.KS, MU).

headline: Cerebras (CBRS) closes +68% on debut at $311.07 — ~$95B market cap on $185 IPO; opened $350, peaked $386
summary: Cerebras Systems opened at $350 on Nasdaq Thursday vs the $185 IPO price, peaked at $386 intraday, and closed at $311.07 (+68% from IPO) — implying ~$95B market cap. The 30M-share offering raised $5.55B, the largest US tech IPO since Uber 2019 and biggest pureplay AI IPO ever. Watch for: (1) follow-on dilution risk and lockup expiry, (2) read-through to NVDA on alt-AI architecture validation — wafer-scale WSE-3 customers include OpenAI and Amazon, (3) sentiment lift for next AI IPO candidates (Anthropic, xAI, Groq private valuations). Open-ended question whether the public-market re-rating of wafer-scale economics pressures clustered-GPU pricing power into 2027.

headline: KOSPI crashes -6.12% Friday after briefly topping 8,000; SK Hynix -7.66% to KRW 1.82M; Samsung -8.6% on strike confirmation
summary: The benchmark KOSPI plunged 488.23 points (-6.12%) to close at 7,493.18 after hitting an intraday record 8,046.78 — the index's largest one-day drop in years. Foreigners dumped 5.6 trillion won of local shares (7th consecutive session of net selling), institutions sold 1.7 trillion won. SK Hynix (000660.KS) slid 7.66% to KRW 1.82M, just one session after breaching the historic KRW 2M handle. Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) plunged 8.6% after its labor union reaffirmed plans for an 18-day strike beginning May 21 (~$700M/day potential cost, $26.8B cumulative impact estimate from industry watchers). Profit-taking on HBM-cycle winners and renewed Middle East tensions amplified the move. Combined SamSung + SK Hynix KOSPI200 weight had topped 50% earlier in the week — concentration unwind risk realized. Risk-off into Friday US open; pressures NVDA/AMD pre-market on memory-supply uncertainty for AI accelerators.

headline: Nvidia -2.6% pre-market as Beijing reportedly pressures Chinese buyers to defer H200 orders; Trump-Xi summit closes with no formal chip deal
summary: NVDA traded $229.51 in pre-market, down 2.64% from Thursday's close, as reports surfaced that Beijing is quietly pressuring Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, and JD.com to defer or block H200 orders pending Chinese government review — mirroring the earlier H20 dynamic. Each of the ~10 cleared buyers can purchase up to 75,000 H200s under US licensing, but zero shipments have executed. Adding pressure: emerging reporting that the Commerce framework requires Nvidia to remit 25% of approved-China H200 sales to the US Treasury. Trump-Xi summit wrapped Friday in Beijing with warm rhetoric and a 200-jet Boeing order (BA +pre-market) but no formal AI/chip-export agreement or rare-earth flow restoration. Treasury's Bessent confirmed delegations are 'discussing AI guardrails' but nothing concrete announced.

headline: HSBC upgrades Cisco to Buy, target $137; Raymond James lifts Arista to Outperform with $164 target — networking AI tape extends
summary: Two notable Friday upgrades reinforce the AI-networking momentum off Wednesday's Cisco $9B FY26 AI-orders guide: HSBC upgraded CSCO to Buy with $137 PT, and Raymond James moved ANET to Outperform from Neutral at $164. UBS separately lifted CSCO target to $132 on the same AI-order strength (raised target multiple from 20x to 24x). Read-through positive to broader AI-networking/optics group (AVGO custom-ASIC partnerships, COHR, LITE optical pull-through, MRVL into May 27 print). Sets up Arista's June quarter print as a key sentiment hurdle for hyperscaler networking allocation.

headline: Samsung union confirms 18-day strike starting May 21; HBM and foundry capacity at risk just as AI memory cycle peaks
summary: After government-mediated talks collapsed earlier this week, the National Samsung Electronics Union (NSEU, ~90,000 members or >70% of Korea workforce) confirmed an 18-day strike starting May 21. Last April's one-day walkout cut memory fab output 18% and foundry output 58% on affected shifts; an 18-day stoppage would land squarely on Nvidia Rubin / AMD MI400 HBM4 qualifications. Trendforce flags the immediate revenue hit as 'contained' but says order risk could shift to SK Hynix (000660.KS) and Micron (MU), and HBM contract pricing could firm further. Industry estimates run from KRW 40T ($28B) base case up to KRW 100T worst case if recovery operations slip. Catalyst path: weekend last-mile talks, then strike effect visible in late-May HBM spot prints.

headline: Powell's term as Fed chair ends today; Kevin Warsh sworn in. Last Powell-led FOMC held at 3.50-3.75% with three dissenters wanting cuts
summary: Jerome Powell's second four-year term as Fed Chair ends May 15 (he stays as a Governor through January 2028). Kevin Warsh — Senate-confirmed May 13 — becomes Chair. Powell's final FOMC (April 29) held rates at 3.50-3.75% with three dissents (incl. Kashkari) preferring a cut, suggesting Warsh inherits a board already tilting dovish heading into the June meeting. Bond-friendly handoff is supportive for long-duration AI growth multiples, partially offsetting the +1.4% MoM PPI shock from May 13 and the inflation backdrop. April retail sales +0.5% MoM (control group +0.5%) printed in-line yesterday. No scheduled Powell or Warsh speech today; focus shifts to Warsh's first communications next week.

headline: Goldman Sachs raises ASML price target to EUR 1,600 on AI-driven EUV demand; ASML now expects 60 low-NA EUV shipments in 2026 vs 80-cap in 2027
summary: GS lifted ASML target to EUR 1,600 (from EUR 1,570) ahead of the Friday US session, citing the same AI lithography supercycle that drove the post-Q1 raise (FY26 net sales now EUR 36-40B vs prior EUR 34-39B). CFO Roger Dassen confirmed 2026 low-NA EUV shipments will hit 60 (+25% YoY) with capacity to ship 80 in 2027. EUV bottleneck remains the binding constraint on 2nm TSMC/Samsung/Intel ramps — implies the WFE-cycle re-rate (AMAT +8% AH, Citi/Lynx/RBC raises) extends to litho. Combined with the AMAT +30% WFE-growth call last night, the Street's 2026-27 semicap revenue assumptions are moving materially higher.

headline: AMD slips after second valuation downgrade in two weeks: Daiwa cut to Outperform May 13, HSBC moved to Hold May 4 — P/E ~33x 2027 EPS leaves no room for miss
summary: AMD trades modestly weaker into Friday after Daiwa Capital downgraded to Outperform on May 13 (third sell-side cut in a fortnight) following HSBC's May 4 move to Hold. Core thesis: AMD's forward valuation expanded from ~19x to ~33x estimated 2027 EPS after the post-Q1 rally and 20+ price-target hikes; insiders have sold ~$54.6M over the past three months; gaming segment guided -20% in H2 on memory/component cost inflation (a direct read-through to the Samsung/SK Hynix HBM cycle). Note the irony: AMD is among the biggest beneficiaries of the AI memory rally that's now squeezing its gaming margins. Cerebras IPO public-market validation of alt-architectures also feeds the 'narrative competition' concern. MI400/HBM4 ramp is the offsetting positive but priced in.

headline: TSMC April 2026 revenue NT$410.7B (+17.5% YoY); YTD growth ~30%, FY26 guide reaffirmed at >30% USD growth and $52-56B capex
summary: TSMC reported April 2026 consolidated revenue of NT$410.73B (US$12.6B), +17.5% YoY, -1.1% MoM. Jan-Apr cumulative revenue ~NT$1.55T, ~+30% YoY. The print reaffirms the FY26 guide for >30% USD revenue growth and 2026 capex at the upper end of the $52-56B range (per April 16 earnings call). HPC (incl. AI/5G) was 61% of Q1 revenue. Print is positive for the WFE complex (AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML — capex flowing) and for AI accelerator supply visibility (NVDA Rubin, AMD MI400, GOOGL TPU v7). Read-through positive for ENTG/MKSI process consumables and AMKR/ASX advanced-packaging.
